summary: This skill implements adaptive echo cancellation using autocorrelation analysis and digital signal processing techniques to remove delayed echo artifacts from synthetic audio recordings, with quality assessment and visualization capabilities.
preconditions: Validate input signal format and range before processing
Handle cases where no significant echo is detected (return original signal)
Implement bounds checking for delay and attenuation parameters
strategy: **Load and validate audio data** - Read .npy file containing normalized 1D audio signal and verify format
**Perform autocorrelation analysis** - Calculate normalized autocorrelation to identify echo delay and attenuation characteristics
**Detect echo parameters** - Find secondary peaks in autocorrelation to determine delay (samples) and attenuation factor
AVOID: Autocorrelation window too short: Use at least 2x expected maximum delay to capture echo peaks properly
AVOID: False peak detection: Filter autocorrelation results and use minimum peak prominence to avoid noise artifacts
AVOID: Over-cancellation artifacts: Limit attenuation factor estimation to realistic range (0.2-0.7) to prevent signal distortion
